applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with inductor tuning
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') with coordinate_descent_tuning and freezing enabled on ConvTranspose2d module

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton kernel with naive implementation
attempted_but_failed.error: Naive element-wise kernel too slow due to excessive kernel launches and nested loops

attempted_but_failed.name: channels_last memory format
attempted_but_failed.error: Overhead of format conversion outweighed any benefit from avoiding transpose kernels

notes: MIOpen's igemm_bwd implementation for ConvTranspose2d is highly optimized
Custom Triton kernel approach is challenging due to complex indexing semantics
torch.compile provides minimal benefit for single conv_transpose2d operation
The operation is already compute-bound with good GPU utilization (~95%)
channels_last format actually hurt performance for this problem size
Further optimization would require algorithmic reformulation or shape-specific tuning

global_notes: ConvTranspose2d is implemented in MIOpen as igemm_bwd which is highly optimized
Beating MIOpen requires either: (1) algorithmic reformulation, (2) better memory layout for specific shapes, or (3) hardware-specific tuning
For Level 1 problems, torch.compile is usually sufficient to achieve baseline+ performance
Score of 60 achieved with marginal but consistent speedup
